data.title: CryptoQuant CEO: Ethereum has internal problems, the team may no longer trust the leadership
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CryptoQuant founder Ki Young Ju retweeted Vitalik Buterin's tweet claiming that "Ethereum needs a lot of young blood" on the X platform, saying that Ethereum is facing risks, that Ethereum's leadership does not respect the team, and that the team or does not trust the leadership. Skilled developers are developing elsewhere. Ethereum's leaders want to exit, but there is no clear successor. Vitalik Buterin's words revealed that Ethereum has internal problems.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Internal Challenges Amid Leadership Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, shared a tweet by Vitalik Buterin on the X platform, highlighting concerns about Ethereum's internal dynamics. Buterin emphasized the need for new talent within the Ethereum community, suggesting underlying issues within the organization.

Ju pointed out several risks facing Ethereum, including a lack of respect between the leadership and the team, leading to potential distrust. He also noted that skilled developers are choosing to work elsewhere, which could impact Ethereum's growth and innovation.

Additionally, Ju mentioned that Ethereum's leaders are considering stepping down, but there is no clear successor in place. This uncertainty could pose challenges for the platform's future stability and direction. Ju's comments reflect a broader concern about Ethereum's internal cohesion and leadership structure.

data.title: YZi Labs: Opening project application portal to long-term builders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ella Zhang, head of YZi Labs, forwarded CZ's content about "considering supporting projects that adopt new token models" and said that YZi Labs is now opening the project application entrance to interested long-term builders, and applicants will receive priority review from YZi Labs.

data.title: Total Bitcoin balance held by short-term BTC holders drops to lowest level since October 2024
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Trading View data, the total balance of Bitcoin held by short-term BTC holders has dropped to the lowest level since October 2024 and is still on a downward trend, which may indicate an overall correction in BTC prices.

data.title: TD Sequential indicator shows a buy signal on the DOGE daily chart, which could indicate a price rebound
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to on-chain analyst Ali's monitoring, the TD Sequential indicator shows a buy signal on the DOGE daily chart, indicating that prices may rebound.

data.title: Bitcoin Active Addresses Surge, Indicating Potential Market Shift
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin's active addresses have reached a near three-month high, suggesting a possible market capitulation that could lead to a price reversal following the recent correction. Data from Glassnode reveals that active addresses on the Bitcoin network surged to over 912,300 on February 28, a level not observed since December 16, 2024, when Bitcoin was valued at approximately $105,000.

The increase in active addresses may indicate a 'capitulation moment' for the cryptocurrency market, as noted by the crypto intelligence platform IntoTheBlock. The firm highlighted in a February 28 post that historically, spikes in on-chain activity have often aligned with market peaks and bottoms, driven by panic sellers exiting and opportunistic buyers entering the market. While no single metric can guarantee a price reversal, this surge suggests the market could be at a critical turning point. In financial markets, capitulation refers to investors selling their positions in panic, leading to a significant price decline and signaling an imminent market bottom before the start of the next uptrend.

Bitcoin's ability to maintain a position above the $80,500 mark is crucial to avoid further losses. Stella Zlatareva, dispatch editor at digital asset investment platform Nexo, emphasized that Bitcoin's ability to reclaim $80,500 will be a key factor in determining near-term momentum. A breakout above this level could pave the way for further upside, while failing to establish it as support may lead to further testing on the downside. Bitcoin may revisit this crucial support if its price falls below $84,000. A potential correction below $84,000 could trigger over $1 billion worth of leveraged long liquidations across all exchanges, according to CoinGlass data.

Despite short-term volatility, Bitcoin's price is closer to forming a market bottom than reaching a local top, as indicated by Bitcoin's market value to realized value (MVRV) Z-score. This technical indicator is used to determine whether an asset is overbought or oversold. On March 1, Bitcoin's MVRV Z-score stood at 2.01, signaling that Bitcoin's price is approaching the green territory at the bottom of the chart, becoming increasingly oversold, according to Glassnode data.

data.title: Bitcoin Holdings of Short-Term Investors Reach Lowest Level Since 2024
data.description: According to Odaily, data from Trading View indicates that the total balance of Bitcoin held by short-term investors has dropped to its lowest level since October 2024. This trend continues to decline, suggesting a potential overall price correction for Bitcoin.

data.title: A whale sold 531.7 billion PEPE in the past hour, losing $7.02 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale sold 531.7 billion PEPE in exchange for 4.1 million DAI in the past hour, with a loss of 7.02 million US dollars. The whale spent 11.1 million US dollars on PEPE in October 2024.
In addition, the whale also sold 88 million BEAM in exchange for 709,000 DAI. The address also holds 17.36 million BEAM, equivalent to about 1.41 million US dollars. It previously bought BEAM for 9.28 million US dollars and lost 7.15 million US dollars.

data.title: Ministry of Public Security Cyber ​​Security Bureau: Beware of fraudulent imitation "DeepSeek" blockchain projects and tokens
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official account of the Cyber ​​Security Bureau of the Ministry of Public Security, fake DeepSeek websites and Trojan viruses have appeared on the Internet one after another, causing great trouble and security risks to the general public. It is reported that the criminal gang launched the "DeepSeek" blockchain project, claiming to be a distributed ledger project developed based on DeepSeek technology, attracting many investors to participate in crowdfunding. After raising a large amount of funds, the team disappeared. Recently, many people have been pulled into the so-called "DeepSeek official group". Some people in the group claimed that they could provide "internal test qualifications" or "advanced courses" and created the virtual currency "DeepSeekCoin", which attracted a large number of investors to buy. As a result, the funds invested by investors were wasted. The Internet police reminded to take precautions, download genuine DeepSeek from the official website and mainstream application markets, and be alert to various security risks to avoid the possibility of downloading counterfeit programs from the source.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Transfers 90 ETH to New Address
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain monitoring platform Arkham reported that five hours ago, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin transferred 90 ETH, approximately valued at $200,000, to the address '0x0BE724A47f5b44E8068F0c4e899c1C8565bb8eb7.'
